label: fiction
title: The Emberline Bakery Fire
charge: Arson with intent to defraud an insurer
elements: The fire was deliberately set, not accidental.
The accused set the fire or caused it to be set.
The accused acted intending to collect on an insurance claim.

beats.text: A neighbour testifies: about an hour before the smoke, she saw the bakery's owner leave through the back lane carrying a red fuel can.
beats.reveal_note: The red can held water for the community garden two doors down, and the neighbour's kitchen clock was later shown to run 90 minutes fast. A vivid image that falls apart on the details.

beats.text: The fire investigator's report: the burn originated inside the walk-in freezer's compressor wiring. No trace of accelerant was found anywhere on the premises.
beats.reveal_note: An electrical point of origin with zero accelerant is extremely hard to reconcile with deliberate arson. The quiet, technical exhibit that actually decides the case.

beats.text: Bank statements show the bakery was three months behind on rent and carrying heavy debt.
beats.reveal_note: Debt is a motive story, not a method. Struggling businesses burn down by accident at least as often as they are torched — the number proves nothing about how this fire started.

beats.text: An insurance clerk testifies that the bakery's fire policy had lapsed 11 days earlier for non-payment. Any claim would have paid nothing.
beats.reveal_note: If there is no payout to collect, the entire fraud theory has no engine. One of the strongest facts in the case, and the easiest to skim straight past.

beats.text: The judge instructs the jury: motive alone is not evidence that a crime took place. The prosecution must prove beyond reasonable doubt that the fire was set deliberately.
beats.reveal_note: Not a fact about the fire, but it tells you how to weigh the rest: the origin report outranks the debt story.

twist: The story writes itself — a drowning business, a red can, a fire. But the blaze started in failed freezer wiring, left no accelerant, and the insurance had already lapsed. There was nothing to collect. The obvious arsonist was just an unlucky baker.
